title: Leontodon boryi
taxonomy: Leontodon boryi is classified within the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. This classification is based on the plant's characteristics and its evolutionary relationships with other species. Leontodon boryi belongs to the tribe Cichorieae, which is a group of plants within the Asteraceae family. The genus Leontodon is also part of the Cichorieae tribe, and it is comprised of various species of dandelion-like plants. Leontodon boryi is a specific species within this genus.

intro: Leontodon boryi, also known as the Bory's hawkbit, is a species of flowering plant in the dandelion genus. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making it useful in the treatment of various ailments such as arthritis, digestive issues, and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Leontodon boryi include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, Leontodon boryi is used to make herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used in the treatment of digestive issues and as a natural anti-inflammatory agent.
